spacey-jane.bio: Fremantle, Australia's indie-rock heart-tuggers, touring third LP 'If That Makes Sense' (2025) — a shoegazy, Mike Crossey-produced step up. Expect mass singalongs on 'Booster Seat' and 'All the Noise'.

news.t: Rolling Stone AU reviews 'If That Makes Sense'
Melodic Mag on the band's ambitious third album

spiritual-cramp.bio: San Francisco punks blending '77 snot, mod swagger and dub bounce. Sophomore LP 'Rude' (Oct 2025, prod. John Congleton) features a Sharon Van Etten duet — live they're a nonstop, amp-hopping party.

news.t: KQED: SF punks poised to blow up with 'Rude'
AltPress goes behind the making of 'Rude'

steph-strings.bio: Melbourne percussive-fingerstyle guitar phenom mixing coastal indie, blues and Celtic flourishes. Self-released debut 'Feel Alive' (Jan 2026) hit No. 2 on the ARIA chart mid first world tour.

news.t: Billboard: Steph Strings wows Brooklyn on world tour
Debut album 'Feel Alive' announced with 'Devil Woman'

steve-cory-are-dead.bio: The mystery booking of the weekend: an evening Where Stage DJ set from a duo with almost no public footprint. Bonnaroo's tiny-font wild cards often become the farm's best inside joke — show up and find out.

sub-focus.bio: UK drum & bass titan Nick Douwma, fresh off 'Contact' (Nov 2025) — a space-themed 14-tracker with Grimes, Katy B and John Summit — and a history-making run as the first D&B artist to headline Red Rocks.

news.t: EDM.com: 'Contact' pushes drum & bass into the cosmos
Sub Focus on D&B's US rise and his new album

sunami.bio: San Jose's 'Real Bay Shit' bruisers — started as a joke, now one of hardcore's biggest draws. 2025 brought the 'Coast to Coast' split with Pain of Truth; their pits are legendarily violent, so brace yourself.

news.t: No Echo: Sunami and Pain of Truth join forces on split

tash-sultana.bio: Melbourne's one-person loop-station marvel, back to solo roots with the 'Return to the Roots' EP (2025) and a world tour after selling out Red Rocks in minutes. Guitar, trumpet, beats — all built live in front of you.

news.t: Tash Sultana shares 'Hold On,' maps US summer tour
First Australian headline shows in six years announced

teddy-swims.bio: Georgia's tattooed soul powerhouse behind 'Lose Control,' touring 'I've Tried Everything But Therapy (Part 2)' (2025) with Giveon, Coco Jones and GloRilla features. That voice hits even harder in a field.

news.t: Variety reviews 'I've Tried Everything But Therapy Pt 2'

tedeschi-trucks-band.bio: Derek Trucks and Susan Tedeschi's 12-piece blues-rock institution hits the farm behind sixth LP 'Future Soul' (March 2026, Fantasy) — slide-guitar transcendence and one of the best live bands in America, full stop.

news.t: TTB announce 'Future Soul' album and 2026 tour
35+ Future Soul tour dates set behind new album

the-chats.bio: Sunshine Coast pub-punks who turned 'Smoko' into a global mullet anthem. Eamon Sandwith and co. play fast, dumb-smart and hilarious — 90 seconds a song, zero pretension, maximum shed-rock chaos.

news.t: Rolling Stone AU catches up with The Chats

the-dare.bio: NYC indie-sleaze ringleader Harrison Patrick Smith — 'Girls,' Charli XCX's 'Guess' — kept 2025 rolling with the 'Afters' deluxe and 'freakquencies' dance EP. Suit, sunglasses, sweat: a filthy-fun dance party.

news.t: Clash: The Dare shares new single 'LCA'
The Dare gives ACL a taste of revamped indie sleaze

the-neighbourhood.bio: The 'Sweater Weather' crew ended a multi-year hiatus with '(((((ultraSOUND)))))' (Nov 2025), their first LP in five years, plus a 2026 deluxe and world tour. Moody California alt-pop built for golden hour.

news.t: Rolling Stone: first Neighbourhood album in five years
Deluxe '(((((ultraSOUND)))))+' lands ahead of world tour

the-runarounds.bio: Wilmington, NC rockers formed for the Outer Banks creators' Prime Video series — the show was axed after one season, but the real-life band plays on, touring and writing a debut album of earnest, hooky heartland rock.

news.t: Deadline: 'The Runarounds' canceled, band plays on

the-stews.bio: Southern garage-rock road dogs out of Auburn and Charleston, riding 2024's Wolfgang Zimmerman-produced LP 'Chicken Fight.' Big riffs, beach-bum energy and a tour schedule that basically never stops.

the-strokes.bio: NYC icons in a genuine new era: seventh LP 'Reality Awaits' (Rick Rubin, due July 2026) is their first since 2020, previewed by 'Going Shopping.' Rare US festival dates — with Valensi on a temporary live break.

news.t: NME: 'Reality Awaits' delayed, huge NYC show announced
The Strokes map 2026 world tour around new album

trixie-mattel.bio: Drag Race superstar turned legit club DJ: her Solid Pink Disco party hit Coachella's Do Lab and toured arenas-worth of fans in 2025. Expect disco edits, house anthems and relentless camp.

news.t: Billboard: Solid Pink Disco tour announced

trombone-shorty.bio: New Orleans' brass-funk ambassador Troy Andrews, touring Grammy-nominated 'Second Line Sunday' (2025) — a tribute to his mother released 20 years to the day after Katrina. With Orleans Avenue, it's a nonstop parade.

news.t: NPR: Trombone Shorty's hometown tribute album
Third annual Shorty Gras tour announced for 2026

turnover.bio: Virginia Beach's shapeshifters — emo to dream pop to disco-funk — return with 'Down On Earth' (May 2026), their first album in four years and first self-released after a decade on Run For Cover.

news.t: Turnover announce 'Down On Earth,' share two songs
Band goes independent, returns to DIY roots

turnstile.bio: Baltimore's genre-melting hardcore heroes made history with five Grammy noms for 'Never Enough' (2025), winning Best Rock Album and Best Metal Performance. Their sets are pure communal mosh euphoria.

news.t: Rolling Stone: Turnstile on historic Grammy noms
Turnstile win big at the 2026 Grammys

villanelle.bio: London grunge trio fronted by Gene Gallagher — yes, Liam's son — channeling Alice In Chains and Soundgarden over Britpop swagger. Debut EP 'Measly Means' (2026) backs up the famous name with real volume.

news.t: NME Radar: Villanelle forge their own grungy image
Rolling Stone on Villanelle's nineties-grunge revival

vince-staples.bio: Long Beach's sharpest wit dropped 'Cry Baby' (2026), his first independent album via Loma Vista, after wrapping two acclaimed seasons of Netflix's 'The Vince Staples Show.' Deadpan banter, airtight West Coast sets.

news.t: 'The Vince Staples Show' S2 trailer and release date

waylon-wyatt.bio: Teenage Arkansas country songwriter who went from viral porch videos to Darkroom Records; debut album 'Dustpiles' lands July 2026, with the Wyatt Flores duet 'Didn't Forget' already a calling card.

news.t: Waylon Wyatt announces debut album 'Dustpiles'

wednesday.bio: Asheville's countrygaze storytellers led by Karly Hartzman, with 'Bleeds' (Sept 2025) hailed by NPR as a rock-album-of-the-year contender. Pedal steel, blown-out fuzz and devastating Southern detail.

news.t: NPR: 'Bleeds' might be the rock album of the year
NME review: hard-won catharsis and thrilling noise

wet-leg.bio: Isle of Wight pranksters gone full rock band: 'Moisturizer' (July 2025) topped the UK chart and bagged three Grammy noms. Rhian Teasdale leads a louder, sweatier live show than the 'Chaise Longue' days.

news.t: Treble reviews Wet Leg's bolder 'Moisturizer'
The Dare remixes 'mangetout' for the dancefloor

wolfmother.bio: Andrew Stockdale's Aussie retro-rockers are playing their five-times-platinum 2005 debut front to back on a 20th-anniversary 2026 tour — 'Woman,' 'Joker & the Thief' and 'Dimension' guaranteed.

news.t: Wolfmother to play debut album in full on 2026 tour

wyatt-flores.bio: Stillwater, Oklahoma red-dirt riser whose breakout keeps snowballing: 'Live at Cain's Ballroom' arrived Feb 2026 and studio album 'Scared of Heights' lands in July. Raw, confessional, huge with young country fans.

news.t: Wyatt Flores announces 'Scared of Heights' album
First live album captures sold-out Cain's Ballroom run

yungblud.bio: Doncaster's rock evangelist rode 2025's 'Idols' to three Grammy noms and won Best Rock Performance for 'Changes' at Ozzy Osbourne's final show — dedicating it to Ozzy. 'Idols II' is on the way; expect total chaos.

news.t: Rolling Stone: Yungblud on 'Idols' II and honoring Ozzy
Yungblud's 'Changes' wins Best Rock Performance Grammy

zack-fox.bio: Atlanta comedian-rapper-DJ ('Jesus Is the One') whose UWAY party series toured North America into 2026, plus an acclaimed turn in Sundance thriller 'Lurker.' Expect jokes woven between house, jungle and rap edits.

news.t: RA: Zack Fox details 2025 North American tour
